name: SOL Foundation Annual Symposium 2024
description: Annual academic symposium at Stanford University covering materials analysis, consciousness research, phenomenology, and UAP policy. Features presentations by scientists, intelligence professionals, and policy researchers. The 2024 symposium continued the series established in 2022.

name: SOL Foundation Annual Symposium 2023
description: Inaugural annual symposium of the SOL Foundation. Featured presentations by Garry Nolan on materials analysis, Peter Skafish on UAP phenomenology, and policy discussions with former intelligence officials. Proceedings published openly.

name: SCU Annual Conference 2023
description: Annual technical conference of the Scientific Coalition for UAP Studies. Features peer-reviewed paper presentations covering radar analysis, physics modeling, materials forensics, and sensor methodology. Proceedings published on explorescu.org.

name: SCU Annual Conference 2022
description: SCU's annual technical conference featuring case analyses and peer-reviewed research presentations. Proceedings published.

name: House Oversight Subcommittee UAP Hearing
location: Rayburn House Office Building, Washington D.C.
description: Historic public congressional hearing featuring sworn testimony from David Grusch (former NGA/NRO intelligence officer), David Fravor (USS Nimitz pilot), and Ryan Graves (former F/A-18 pilot). Grusch alleged the existence of a non-human intelligence crash retrieval program. Most-watched UAP congressional event to date.

name: Senate Armed Services Committee UAP Hearing
description: Senate hearing on UAP organized by Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, featuring testimony from AARO director Sean Kirkpatrick. Discussed AARO's reporting mechanisms, the scope of UAP investigations, and the establishment of the secure reporting system for government personnel.

name: Senate Intelligence Committee UAP Closed Briefing
description: Closed-session Senate Intelligence Committee briefing followed by the first open UAP hearing in 54 years. Deputy Secretary of Defense Ronald Moultrie and OUSD(I&S) Director Scott Bray testified publicly. Bray presented video of an unidentified metallic sphere captured by a Navy F/A-18.

name: AIAA SciTech Forum 2024
organizer_name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description: Major aerospace engineering conference with UAP-adjacent sessions on advanced propulsion and anomalous aerial vehicle analysis. AIAA has increasingly included UAP-relevant technical sessions as academic interest grows.

name: SOL Foundation Annual Symposium 2025
description: The 4th annual SOL Foundation symposium at Stanford University. Continued the series with presentations spanning materials analysis, consciousness research, UAP phenomenology, and disclosure policy. Organized by Garry Nolan and the SOL Foundation leadership team.

name: Galileo Project Science Conference
description: Galileo Project's public scientific conference presenting data from the Harvard Yard Observatory deployment. Avi Loeb and team presented analysis of approximately 1 million detected objects, with a small subset showing anomalous properties. First major data release from the project.

name: SSE Annual Conference 2025
description: Annual conference of the Society for Scientific Exploration featuring peer-reviewed presentations on anomalistics, parapsychology, UAP research, and consciousness studies. One of the few academic conferences where UAP, psi, and consciousness research are presented alongside one another to a scientific audience. The SSE annual conference series dates to the organization's founding in 1982.

name: SSE Annual Conference 2026
description: Annual conference of the Society for Scientific Exploration. Peer-reviewed paper presentations on anomalistics, parapsychology, UAP research, and consciousness studies. Call for papers typically opens in early spring.

name: Parapsychological Association Annual Conference 2025
description: Annual convention of the Parapsychological Association, the international professional body for psi research affiliated with the AAAS. Peer-reviewed paper presentations covering ESP, psychokinesis, anomalous cognition, near-death experiences, and related topics. The PA's annual convention is among the highest-credentialed academic venues for parapsychology research.

name: Society for Psychical Research Annual Conference 2025
description: Annual conference of the Society for Psychical Research, the world's oldest scientific organization for psychical research. Features academic paper presentations on apparitions, telepathy, NDEs, anomalous cognition, and related topics. The SPR annual conference has been a continuous fixture since the 1880s, representing the longest-running academic series in the field.

name: IANDS Annual Conference 2025
description: Annual conference of the International Association for Near-Death Studies. Features research presentations on near-death experiences, shared death experiences, deathbed visions, and related consciousness phenomena. Attended by researchers, clinicians, and experiencers. The IANDS conference bridges clinical NDE research with broader consciousness science and increasingly intersects with UAP contact phenomenology.

name: IRVA Annual Conference 2025
description: Annual conference of the International Remote Viewing Association. Presentations cover controlled remote viewing methodology, research results, historical program retrospectives on the SRI/CIA-funded Stargate program, and applications. The IRVA conference maintains the research lineage of the U.S. government's remote viewing program, which ran from 1972 to 1995.

name: MUFON International Symposium 2025
description: Annual international symposium of the Mutual UFO Network, the largest civilian UAP investigation organization. Presentations cover recent case investigations, methodology, data analysis from MUFON's reporting database, and broader UAP research. The MUFON symposium has been a fixture of civilian UAP research since the 1970s.

name: BUFORA Annual Conference 2025
description: Annual conference of the British UFO Research Association. Presentations cover recent UK UAP cases, investigation methodology, and archival research. BUFORA's annual conference is the primary UK civilian venue for UAP research and has convened since the 1960s.
